title: Eriosyce paucicostata
taxonomy: Eriosyce paucicostata is classified within the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae and is further classified under the tribe Cacteae. Within this tribe, it is placed in the subtribe Cactinae. Eriosyce paucicostata is part of the genus Eriosyce, which is a group of cacti native to South America. The species is native to the coastal regions of Chile.
uses: Eriosyce paucicostata has been traditionally used in folk medicine, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied. It is often employed in the treatment of various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. Some practitioners also use it to support immune function and reduce inflammation. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal benefits and potential side effects. Additionally, its use should be approached with caution due to the lack of comprehensive studies on its pharmacological properties.

intro: Eriosyce paucicostata, also known as the few-spined Eriosyce or few-costate Eriosyce, is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its richness in antioxidants and flavonoids. The bioactive constituents of Eriosyce paucicostata include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as other compounds like phenolic acids and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Eriosyce paucicostata is often used to make herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
